description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a large, rectangular array of solar panels elevated above a patterned platform. The solar panels are supported by a series of angled metal posts, creating a canopy-like structure that provides shade to the area below. The platform beneath the panels is decorated with geometric black, white, and blue patterns, and includes a slightly raised section at one end, possibly for access or maintenance. The structure appears robust, with the solar panels forming the dominant visual element and the support posts spaced evenly for stability. The overall design suggests it is intended for urban environments, such as parking lots, public plazas, or transit stops, where both energy generation and shelter are needed. No visible electrical inverters or cabling are apparent, and the mounting structure is likely made of steel or aluminum.
description: A large, elevated solar panel array supported by angled metal posts over a patterned platform, suitable for urban energy generation and shade.
description_view_0: Front view showing the patterned platform leading up to the solar panel canopy, with the support posts visible underneath.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the solar panel array and the evenly spaced support posts beneath the panels.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back edge of the platform and the underside of the solar panels, with the support structure visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the patterned platform, the raised section at one end, and the full span of the solar panel canopy.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with angled steel or aluminum posts
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 8-10 panels
